parsed_review.summary: CP00-574 is a descriptor-only RP18.P07 contract-implementation-slice pack (40 units, RP18.P07.M02.S03-RP18.P07.M03.S20, risk B). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is honest and fully supported: production_ready and runtime_ready are explicitly false (manifest.json:6,9; command-evidence.json), implementation_layer is "descriptor", and the only positive claim is the "..._descriptor_verified" flag. The implementation is pure frozen-data construction: freezeCp574Result (service.js:2917) sets every dispatch/write/persist/storage/audit/lock flag to false with no_real_data:true and synthetic_only:true, and validateAiLegalWorkflowsCoreCp574* (validators.js:4243,4278) independently enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included:false, no Claude promotion, and no enterprise-trust claim. Pack binding (registry.js:392-407), deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:22,security_audit:4,implementation:3,hermes_evidence:4,fixture:2,test:4,claude_review:1}=40 (hand-verified against baseline units), micro-title split 20/20, all seven required exports, the contract projection (deepEqual at validate-rp18...mjs:1289-1295), README (#cp00-574), manifest, plan, and next-pack-queue are mutually consistent. Source files contain no fs/network/db/runtime operations and import only internal descriptors. Claude is correctly scoped as a read-only, non-final-approver reviewer; human final approval is required and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptor strings (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false; manifest no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true). Review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:705) covers failed auth, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, and missing references; the readiness auth-preflight returned valid JSON (1444B stdout, exit 0). Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, review-receipt.json) are correctly absent pending this review step, consistent with the documented hardening sequence. Unrelated dirty files are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs. One non-blocking P3 nit on the "production_ready_candidate" field name; no P0/P1/P2 issues. The pack may proceed to post-review closeout, with enterprise trust remaining gated on human final approval.

findings.title: Hermes packet field 'production_ready_candidate' name could be misread as a runtime-readiness claim
findings.details: createAiLegalWorkflowsCoreCp574HermesEvidencePacket sets production_ready_candidate = coverage.valid && slice.valid (validators.js:4351) and the validator script asserts it is true (validate-rp18-ai-legal-workflows-core-contract.mjs:1297). The value reflects only descriptor coverage + descriptor-slice validation, not runtime/production readiness. Read in isolation (e.g. by a downstream automated gate or human scanning the Hermes packet), the name could be mistaken for an assertion that the pack is production/runtime ready. In context it is NOT misleading: manifest.json sets production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false (lines 6,9), command-evidence.json sets production_ready:false, the pack binding's production_ready_flag is explicitly the descriptor-verification flag 'ai_legal_workflows_core_p07_contract_implementation_slice_descriptor_verified', and the no-write attestation sets claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. No overclaiming occurs and the standing 'never weaken production_ready gates' directive is honored.
findings.recommendation: Optional, non-blocking: consider renaming the field to something like descriptor_validation_passed to remove any ambiguity for readers consuming the Hermes evidence packet in isolation. This is a program-wide pattern inherited from CP551+, so any rename should be applied consistently rather than only for CP00-574.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as-is; non-blocking nit. The authoritative production_ready and runtime_ready flags are explicitly false and consistent across manifest, command-evidence, and contract, so the descriptor-scoped candidate flag does not constitute a production or enterprise-trust claim. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
